mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending demand contraction, prompting equity investors to price in lower future earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising housing starts and expanding retail sales credit indicates synchronized consumer spending strength, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve model fit beyond single-series proxies, or forward returns are flat when both series rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds its historical trend while inflation remains elevated, supply-side bottlenecks force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Industrial production breaks above the threshold but equity markets rally instead of declining, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, providing fundamental support that translates into positive forward equity returns over a quarterly window regardless of short-term volatility.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP acceleration consistently correlates with negative or flat 60-day forward returns, or the rolling correlation coefficient remains below 0.10 over a decade.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in the consumer price index signals persistent inflationary pressure that erodes real yields and forces monetary policy to stay restrictive, depressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: CPI accelerations above the threshold are followed by positive equity returns in > 55% of cases, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns when both conditions align, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power over PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts below the 1400 thousand unit threshold indicates weakening construction activity and consumer confidence, which precedes broader economic slowdown and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Housing starts falling below the threshold are consistently followed by positive equity returns, or the signal's hit rate over a 10-year sample does not exceed 55%.
